title: "Banded Dotterel Stable Isotopes"
subtitle: | 
  Exploration of dataset
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    self-contained: true
    highlight-style: github
    theme: Cosmo
execute:
  warning: false
  cache: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(cache = TRUE)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, results='hide', warning=FALSE, results='hide', include=FALSE, cache=FALSE}
## Prerequisites

### R packages
# Set a CRAN mirror before installing packages
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"))
# -   The following packages are needed for analysis and can be easily installed from [CRAN](http://cran.r-project.org/) or GitHub by running the following code chunk:

# a vector of all the packages needed in the project
packages_required_in_project <- c("V8", "concaveman",
                                  "tidyverse",
                                  "readxl",
                                  "RMark",
                                  "RColorBrewer",
                                  "patchwork",
                                  "mapview",
                                  "lubridate",
                                  # "extrafont",
                                  "here",
                                  "DT",
                                  "leaflet",
                                  "sf",
                                  "leafpop",
                                  "tsibble",
                                  "corrplot",
                                  "gghalves",
                                  "gam",
                                  "pscl",
                                  "gamlss",
                                  "gt",
                                  "lme4",
                                  "ggpattern",
                                  "gtsummary",
                                  "effects",
                                  "lattice",
                                  "rptR",
                                  "partR2",
                                  "broom.mixed",
                                  "forcats",
                                  "glmmTMB",
                                  "ggmap",
                                  "MuMIn",
                                  "googlesheets4", 
                                  "sf",
                                  "sp", 
                                  "rnaturalearth", 
                                  "rnaturalearthdata", 
                                  "fuzzyjoin", "gstat", "conflicted")

# of the required packages, check if some need to be installed
new.packages <- 
  packages_required_in_project[!(packages_required_in_project %in% 
                                   installed.packages()[,"Package"])]

# install all packages that are not locally available
if(length(new.packages)) install.packages(new.packages)

# load all the packages into the current R session
lapply(packages_required_in_project, require, character.only = TRUE)

# set the home directory to where the project is locally based (i.e., to find 
# the relevant datasets to import, etc.
here::set_here()

# Use gs4_auth() to log in and authenticate with your Google account. 
# You can specify your email address to ensure the correct account is used. 
# Once authenticated, you won't need to select the account interactively when using read_sheet()
gs4_auth(email = "luke.eberhart@gmail.com")

conflicted::conflict_prefer("select", "dplyr")
conflicted::conflict_prefer("filter", "dplyr")
conflicted::conflict_prefer("lmer", "lme4")
conflicted::conflict_prefer("accumulate", "purrr")
conflicted::conflict_prefer("ar", "brms")
conflicted::conflict_prefer("Beta", "brms")
# conflicted::conflict_prefer("calibration", "rstan")
conflicted::conflict_prefer("area", "MASS")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, results='hide', warning=FALSE, include=FALSE, eval=FALSE, cache=FALSE}
### Plotting themes

# -   The following plotting themes, colors, and typefaces are used throughout the project:

# define the plotting theme to be used in subsequent ggplots
luke_theme <-
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(family = "Verdana"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "grey40"),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    panel.border = element_rect(linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
    legend.position.inside = c(0.1, 0.9)
  )
```

### custom functions

```{r}
# Function to extract month from date strings
extract_month <- function(date_str) {
  if (is.na(date_str)) {
    return(NA)
  }
  
  # Handle dates like "16/07/1887"
  if (grepl("^\\d{2}/\\d{2}/\\d{4}$", date_str)) {
    return(month(dmy(date_str)))
  }
  
  # Handle dates like "1974-07-26"
  if (grepl("^\\d{4}-\\d{1,2}-\\d{1,2}$", date_str)) {
    return(month(ymd(date_str)))
  }
  
  # Handle dates like "32118" (Excel date format)
  if (grepl("^\\d{4,5}$", date_str)) {
    return(month(as.Date(as.numeric(date_str), origin = "1899-12-30")))
  }
  
  # Handle 4-character year strings
  if (nchar(date_str) == 4) {
    return(NA)
  }
  
  return(NA)
}

# Function to extract year from date strings (handles Excel serials like "8753" correctly)
extract_year <- function(date_str) {
  if (is.na(date_str)) return(NA_real_)
  date_str <- trimws(as.character(date_str))
  
  # If string is only digits (no letters, punctuation)
  if (grepl("^\\d+$", date_str)) {
    num <- as.numeric(date_str)
    # If it's a plausible 4-digit calendar year, return it directly
    if (num >= 1800 && num <= 2100) {
      return(as.numeric(num))
    }
    # Otherwise treat as Excel serial date number and convert
    if (num >= 0 && num < 100000) {
      dt <- suppressWarnings(as.Date(num, origin = "1899-12-30"))
      if (!is.na(dt)) return(year(dt))
    }
    # fallback
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  
  # Full ISO-like date "1881-06-28" or "1894-8-30"
  if (grepl("^\\d{4}-\\d{1,2}-\\d{1,2}$", date_str)) {
    parsed <- suppressWarnings(ymd(date_str))
    if (!is.na(parsed)) return(year(parsed))
  }
  
  # DMY numeric slashes "16/07/1887"
  if (grepl("^\\d{1,2}/\\d{1,2}/\\d{4}$", date_str)) {
    parsed <- suppressWarnings(dmy(date_str))
    if (!is.na(parsed)) return(year(parsed))
  }
  
  # Try parsing day-month-year text variants, e.g. "9 May 1899", "26 January, 1892", "19, August, 1897"
  parsed <- suppressWarnings(dmy(gsub(",", "", date_str)))
  if (!is.na(parsed)) return(year(parsed))
  
  # Month-year formats like "Jul 1884", "July, 1874", "Aug 1881"
  if (grepl("[A-Za-z]+[^0-9A-Za-z]*\\d{4}", date_str)) {
    parsed2 <- suppressWarnings(dmy(paste0("01 ", gsub(",", "", date_str))))
    if (!is.na(parsed2)) return(year(parsed2))
  }
  
  # "pre-1944" -> extract 1944 (case-insensitive)
  if (grepl("^pre-\\d{4}$", date_str, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    return(as.numeric(sub("(?i)pre-", "", date_str, perl = TRUE)))
  }
  
  # If there's a standalone 4-digit year anywhere (e.g. "1929 (likely)" or "(1929)")
  if (grepl("\\b\\d{4}\\b", date_str)) {
    yr <- regmatches(date_str, regexpr("\\b\\d{4}\\b", date_str))
    yr_num <- as.numeric(yr)
    # sanity-check year range
    if (!is.na(yr_num) && yr_num >= 1000 && yr_num <= 2100) return(yr_num)
  }
  
  # If nothing matched, return NA
  return(NA_real_)
}
```

# Triplicate analyses

This was conducted to assess the within-individual variation in feathers - we ideally want to be able to homogenize all breast feathers collected to increase the sample material but this assumes that there is limited within-individual variation among breast feathers.

For specimens that had enough material, we took 3 breast feathers and analyzed C, N, and O for each feather. The following analysis assesses the within- vs. among-individual variation in these three isotopes to provide insights on our ability to homogenize the feathers and use these isotopes for spatial inference.

### import and wrangle isotope data

```{r}
# import and consolidate key columns
triplicate_CN_dat <-
  read_excel(here("data/old file versions/Master_SI_Feathers_LEH_Sept24_v1_SB_v3_sample wts added.xls"), 
                     sheet = "Sample results_C&N", 
                     col_types = "text", skip = 10, col_names = TRUE) %>% 
  select(`Index #`, `Identifier 1`, `Amount`, `Ampl  Flash TCD...9`, `All Correct Amt % N Flash TCD`, `Ampl  28`, `normalised d15N`, `avg  Amt% N`, `stdev  Amt% N`, `avg  d15N`, `stdev d15N`, `Ampl  Flash TCD...18`, `All Correct Amt%C Flash TCD`, `Ampl  44`, `normalised d13C`, `Sample Dilution`, `avg  Amt% C`, `stdev  Amt% C`, `avg  d13C`, `stdev d13C`, `C:N Mass ratio`) %>% 
    rename_with(~ .x %>%
                tolower() %>%
                str_replace_all(" ", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("__", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("___", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("%", "_perc_") %>%
                str_replace_all(":", "") %>%
                str_replace_all("#", "")) %>%
  mutate(
    feather = str_sub(identifier_1, -1),
    NewColumn = str_extract(identifier_1, "^[^ ]+"),  # Extract text to the left of the first white space
    identifier_1 = str_trim(str_replace(identifier_1, "^[^ ]+ ", ""))  # Remove text to the left of the first white space and trim remaining white space
  ) %>% mutate(identifier_1 = str_replace(identifier_1, "_.*", "")) %>% 
  rename(Specimen_ID = NewColumn,
         Feather_type = identifier_1) %>% 
  select(Specimen_ID, Feather_type, feather, amount, normalised_d15n, normalised_d13c) %>% 
  mutate(amount = as.numeric(amount), 
         normalised_d15n = as.numeric(normalised_d15n), 
         normalised_d13c = as.numeric(normalised_d13c)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(normalised_d15n, normalised_d13c),
    names_to = "isotope",
    values_to = "value"
  )

# import and consolidate key columns
triplicate_H_dat <-
  read_excel(here("data/Master_TCEA_H_SI_Batch 1_triplicate breast feathers_LEH_Nov25_SB.xls"), 
                     sheet = "Sample results_H", 
                     col_types = "text", skip = 9, col_names = TRUE) %>%
  slice(-11) %>% 
    rename_with(~ .x %>%
                tolower() %>%
                str_replace_all(" ", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("__", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("___", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("%", "_perc_") %>%
                str_replace_all(":", "") %>%
                str_replace_all("#", "")) %>%
  dplyr::select(identifier_1, normalised_d2h) %>% 
  mutate(
    feather = str_extract(identifier_1, "(?<=_)."), 
    Feather_type = "Breast",
    Specimen_ID  = str_extract(identifier_1, "^[^_]+")
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::select(Specimen_ID, Feather_type, feather, normalised_d2h) %>% 
  mutate(normalised_d2h = as.numeric(normalised_d2h)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(normalised_d2h),
    names_to = "isotope",
    values_to = "value"
  )

# import and consolidate key columns
triplicate_O_dat <-
  read_excel(here("data/Master_TCEA_O_SI_Breast Feathers_Replication_Batch 4_LEH_Sept25_SB.xls"), 
                     sheet = "Sample results_O", 
                     col_types = "text", skip = 6, col_names = TRUE) %>%
  slice(-1) %>%
  slice(1:15) %>% 
    rename_with(~ .x %>%
                tolower() %>%
                str_replace_all(" ", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("__", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("___", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("%", "_perc_") %>%
                str_replace_all(":", "") %>%
                str_replace_all("#", "")) %>%
  select(identifier_1, `amount`, 
         ampl_28, normalised_d18o) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(normalised_d18o)) %>%
  filter(identifier_1 != "AV21986_A feather") %>% 
  mutate(
    feather = str_extract(identifier_1, "(?<=_)."), 
    Feather_type = "Breast",
    Specimen_ID  = str_extract(identifier_1, "^[^_]+")
  ) %>% 
  select(Specimen_ID, Feather_type, feather, amount, normalised_d18o) %>% 
  mutate(amount = as.numeric(amount), 
         normalised_d18o = as.numeric(normalised_d18o)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(normalised_d18o),
    names_to = "isotope",
    values_to = "value"
  )

triplicate_CNOH_dat <- 
  bind_rows(triplicate_O_dat, triplicate_CN_dat, triplicate_H_dat)
```

### import and wrangle sample meta data

```{r}
# import meta data for all museum samples and add spatial data.
meta_data <-
  read_excel(here("data/MASTER_museum_ALL SAMPLES INVENTORY_banded_dotterels_v4.xlsx"), 
             col_types = "text") %>% 
  mutate(Specimen_ID_ = str_remove(Specimen_ID, regex(" (Breast|Primary)$", ignore_case = TRUE))) %>%
  mutate(Specimen_ID_ = str_remove_all(Specimen_ID_, regex("AJB ", ignore_case = TRUE)))  %>%
  mutate(Specimen_ID_ = str_remove_all(Specimen_ID_, regex(" -", ignore_case = TRUE)))%>% 
  select(Feather_type, Specimen_ID, Specimen_ID_, General_collection_location, 
         Specific_collection_location, Collection_date) %>% 
  mutate(Month = str_extract(Collection_date, "(Jan|Feb|Mar|Apr|May|Jun|Jul|Aug|Sep|Oct|Nov|Dec)")) %>% 
  mutate(Month = match(tolower(Month), tolower(month.abb))) %>%
  mutate(
    month_extracted = sapply(Collection_date, extract_month),
    Month = ifelse(is.na(Month) & !is.na(month_extracted), month_extracted, Month),
    Year = sapply(Collection_date, extract_year)
  ) %>% 
  select(-month_extracted) %>% 
  mutate(location = paste(Specific_collection_location, General_collection_location, sep = ", ")) %>%
  mutate(location = str_replace_all(location, "NA, ", "")) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Specimen_ID))

# Create a data frame 
df <- data.frame(location = meta_data$location)

# Remove duplicates to avoid redundant API calls
unique_locations <- unique(df$location)

# Get latitude and longitude for unique locations
geocode_data <- geocode(unique_locations)

# Combine the geocode data with the unique locations
location_data <- data.frame(location = unique_locations, geocode_data) %>% filter(!is.na(location))

meta_data_ <-
  left_join(meta_data, location_data, by = "location") %>% 
  select(-c(Feather_type, Specimen_ID)) %>%
  distinct() %>% 
  rename(Specimen_ID = Specimen_ID_)
```

### merge triplicate data with metadata

```{r}
triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta <-
  stringdist_left_join(
    triplicate_CNOH_dat, meta_data_,
    by = "Specimen_ID",
    method = "cosine", # Jaro-Winkler distance
    # max_dist = 1,         # tweak based on how much difference you allow
    distance_col = "dist"
  ) %>% group_by(Specimen_ID.x, Feather_type, feather, isotope) %>% arrange(dist) %>% slice(1) %>%
  select(Specimen_ID.x, Specimen_ID.y, dist, everything()) %>% ungroup() %>% arrange(desc(dist)) %>% 
  select(-c(Specimen_ID.y, dist)) %>% 
  rename(Specimen_ID = Specimen_ID.x) %>% 
  mutate(geo_region = ifelse(str_detect(location, "Australia"), "Australia", 
                             ifelse(str_detect(location, "New Zealand"), "New Zealand", NA))) %>% 
  mutate(geo_region_bin = ifelse(geo_region == "Australia", 1, 0)) %>%
  mutate(
    month_name = month.name[Month],          # full month names
    month_abbrev = month.abb[Month],         # abbreviated names
    month_factor = factor(Month, levels = 1:12, labels = month.name, ordered = TRUE) # ordered factor
  ) %>% 
  mutate(lon = ifelse(lon < 0, lon + 180*2, lon)) %>% 
  mutate(Specific_collection_location = 
           ifelse(is.na(Specific_collection_location), General_collection_location, Specific_collection_location))
```

### calculate hulls for triplicates
C & N hulls
```{r}
# Compute convex hulls for each Specimen_ID using chull() from sp
hulls_triplicates_CN <- triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>%
  filter(!(isotope %in% c("normalised_d18o", "normalised_d2h"))) %>% 
   pivot_wider(
     names_from = isotope,
     values_from = value
   ) %>%
  group_by(Specimen_ID) %>%
  do({
    # Extract the data for the current group
    group_data <- .[ , c("normalised_d13c", "normalised_d15n")]
    
    # Create a spatial object but extract coordinates as a regular matrix
    coords <- SpatialPoints(group_data)
    coords_matrix <- coords@coords
    
    # Compute the convex hull using chull
    chull_indices <- chull(coords_matrix)
    hull_coords <- group_data[chull_indices, ]
    
    # Return the convex hull coordinates
    data.frame(hull = I(list(hull_coords)))
  }) %>%
  unnest(cols = c(hull)) %>% 
  left_join(., triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% 
              dplyr::select(Specimen_ID, Month, location, lon, lat, geo_region, geo_region_bin) %>% distinct(), 
            by = "Specimen_ID")
```

#### among- vs. within-individual variation
```{r}
# install.packages("randomForest")

library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(performance)
library(broom.mixed)
library(caret)
library(MASS)
library(randomForest)
library(patchwork)
library(brms)

# Clean and coerce types
dat <- triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>%
mutate(
Specimen_ID = as.factor(Specimen_ID),
Specific_collection_location = as.factor(Specific_collection_location),
feather = as.factor(feather),
isotope = as.character(isotope),
value = as.numeric(value)
) %>%
filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
  group_by(isotope) %>%
  mutate(value_scaled = as.numeric(scale(value))) %>%
  ungroup()  %>% 
  mutate(
    Month = factor(Month, levels = 1:12, labels = month.abb)
  )

CNOH_dat_meta_full <- readRDS(file = "data/CNOH_museum_samples_201125.rds")
CNOH_dat_meta_full %>% 
  group_by(Specimen_ID, isotope, Feather_type, value) %>% 
  summarise(mean_value = mean(value)) %>% 
  group_by(isotope) %>% 
  summarise(mean_iso_value = mean(mean_value),
            sd_iso_value = sd(mean_value))

triplicate_ids <- 
  CNOH_dat_meta_full %>% 
  filter(!is.na(feather)) %>% 
  pull(Specimen_ID) %>% 
  unique()

triplicate_dat <-
  CNOH_dat_meta_full %>% 
  filter(Specimen_ID %in% triplicate_ids) %>% 
  mutate(value_scaled = ifelse(isotope == "normalised_d13c", (value - -18.8) / 3.67,
                        ifelse(isotope == "normalised_d15n", (value - 10.9) / 2.63,
                        ifelse(isotope == "normalised_d18o", (value - 14.1) / 1.39,
                        ifelse(isotope == "normalised_d2h", (value - -21.6) / 22.0, NA))))) %>%
  mutate(
      Specimen_ID = as.factor(Specimen_ID),
      Specific_collection_location = as.factor(Specific_collection_location),
      feather = as.factor(feather),
      isotope = as.character(isotope),
      value_scaled = as.numeric(value_scaled)
    ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(value_scaled)) %>%
  ungroup()  %>% 
  mutate(
    Month = factor(Month, levels = 1:12, labels = month.abb)
  ) #%>% 
  # pivot_wider(
  #   names_from = isotope,
  #   values_from = value_scaled
  # ) %>% 
  # dplyr::select(Specimen_ID, Feather_type, region, normalised_d13c, normalised_d15n) %>% 
  #   filter(region != "homogenate") %>%
  # group_by(Specimen_ID, Feather_type, region) %>%
  # summarise(
  #   normalised_d13c = coalesce(normalised_d13c[!is.na(normalised_d13c)], NA_real_),
  #   normalised_d15n = coalesce(normalised_d15n[!is.na(normalised_d15n)], NA_real_),
  #   .groups = "drop"
  # )


ggplot() +
  geom_bar(
    data = triplicate_dat %>% dplyr::select(Specimen_ID, Month) %>% ungroup() %>% distinct(),
    aes(x = Month)
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
  # facet_grid(geo_region ~ isotope) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  luke_theme

# --- Helper functions -------------------------------------------------------
get_varcomp_lmer <- function(mod) {
vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod)) %>% dplyr::select(grp, vcov)
names(vc) <- c("group", "variance")
total <- sum(vc$variance)
vc <- vc %>% mutate(prop = variance / total)
vc <- vc %>% arrange(desc(prop))
list(table = vc, total = total)
}


calc_repeatability <- function(mod, group = "Specimen_ID") {
vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod))
var_ind <- vc$vcov[vc$grp == group]
var_total <- sum(vc$vcov)
return(var_ind / var_total)
}

# --- Fit lmer models per isotope --------------------------------------------
lmer_results <- list()
vc_table <- tibble()
icc_table <- tibble()
isotopes <- unique(triplicate_dat$isotope)

for (iso in isotopes) {
message("Fitting lmer for ", iso)
d_iso <- triplicate_dat %>% filter(isotope == iso)


# Ensure there are repeated measures; if not, skip
if (n_distinct(d_iso$Specimen_ID) < 2) {
warning("Not enough individuals for isotope ", iso, " - skipping")
next
}


# Fit model
form <- as.formula("value_scaled ~ 1 + (1 | Specific_collection_location) + (1 | Specimen_ID)")
m <- tryCatch(lmer(form, data = d_iso, REML = TRUE), error = function(e) e)
if (inherits(m, "error")) {
warning("lmer failed for ", iso, ": ", m$message)
next
}


lmer_results[[iso]] <- m
vc <- get_varcomp_lmer(m)$table %>% mutate(isotope = iso)
vc_table <- bind_rows(vc_table, vc)
icc <- calc_repeatability(m, group = "Specimen_ID")
icc_table <- bind_rows(icc_table, tibble(isotope = iso, repeatability = icc))
}


# Tidy vc_table for plotting: ensure four groups present per isotope
vc_plot_df <- vc_table %>%
mutate(group = factor(group, levels = unique(group))) %>%
rename(variance = variance, proportion = prop)

# Tidy vc_table for plotting: ensure four groups present per isotope
vc_plot_df <- vc_table %>%
mutate(group = factor(group, levels = unique(group))) %>%
rename(variance = variance, proportion = prop)


# --- Plot variance partitioning per isotope ---------------------------------
# Prepare a plot dataframe from lmer results (preferred for clarity)
plot_df <- vc_table %>%
rename(component = group) %>%
mutate(component = recode(component,
"Specific_collection_location" = "Population",
"Specimen_ID" = "Individual",
"Residual" = "Residual")) %>%
group_by(isotope) %>%
mutate(component = factor(component, levels = c("Population","Individual","Feather","Residual"))) %>%
ungroup()


# p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = isotope, y = prop, fill = component)) +
#   geom_col(position = "stack") +
#   scale_x_discrete(labels = iso_labels) +
#   labs(
#     x = "Isotope",
#     y = "Proportion of total variance",
#     fill = "Component",
#     title = "Variance partitioning per isotope"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1")
# p

# Create a named vector mapping original isotope names to formatted labels
iso_labels <- c(
  "normalised_d13c" = "delta^{13}*C",
  "normalised_d15n" = "delta^{15}*N",
  "normalised_d2h"  = "delta^{2}*H",
  "normalised_d18o" = "delta^{18}*O"
)

vc_pie_chart <- 
  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = "", y = prop, fill = component)) +
    geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
    coord_polar(theta = "y") +
    facet_grid(isotope ~ ., labeller = labeller(isotope = iso_labels)) +
    labs(
        x = NULL,
        y = NULL,
        fill = "Variance\ncomponent\npartitioning",
        title = "Variance partitioning per isotope"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("grey20", "grey70", "grey90"),
    guide = guide_legend(
      title.position = "top",    # move title above keys
      title.hjust = 0.5,         # center title
      label.position = "right"   # labels stay to the right of keys
    )) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        title = element_blank(),
        
        # Legend customization
        legend.position = "right",        # move legend to the right
        legend.direction = "vertical",    # legend items stacked (default), see below
        # legend.text = element_text(angle = 90)
        # legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", angle = 90),
        # legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"),   # increase spacing between keys
        # legend.key.width  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    
    # Increase white gap between keys
    # legend.spacing.x = unit(5, "cm") 
    )

vc_pie_chart

# Reorder individuals by population
dat_plot <- triplicate_dat %>%
  mutate(
    site = case_when(
      Specific_collection_location == "Waitangi West" ~ "Chatham Is.",
      Specific_collection_location == "Paraparaumu, Wellington" ~ "Wellington",
      Specific_collection_location == "Enderby Island"  ~ "Auckland Is.",
      Specific_collection_location == "Lake Ellesmere, Canterbury" ~ "Lake Ellesmere",
      Specific_collection_location == "Stewart Island, New Zealand"  ~ "Stewart Is.",
      TRUE ~ Specific_collection_location
    ),
    site = factor(site, 
                  levels = c("Auckland Is.", 
                             "Stewart Is.",
                             "Lake Ellesmere", 
                             "Chatham Is.",
                             "Wellington",
                             "Porirua")),
    Specimen_ID = fct_reorder(Specimen_ID, 
                              as.numeric(site), 
                              .fun = min),
    isotope = factor(isotope)
  )



scatterplot <- 
  ggplot(dat_plot, aes(
    x = Specimen_ID,
    y = value_scaled,
    colour = site
  )) +
  geom_jitter(
    size = 2.5,
    alpha = 0.8, width = 0.15
  ) +

  # Mean ± 95% CI per individual
  # stat_summary(
  #   fun = mean,
  #   fun.min = function(z) mean(z) - 1.96 * sd(z) / sqrt(length(z)),
  #   fun.max = function(z) mean(z) + 1.96 * sd(z) / sqrt(length(z)),
  #   geom = "pointrange",
  #   colour = "grey20",
  #   size = 0.4,
  #   position = position_nudge(x = 0.15)  # CI nudged right
  # ) +

  facet_grid(isotope ~ ., scales = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(isotope = as_labeller(iso_labels, label_parsed))) +
  labs(
    x = "Individual",
    y = "Scaled isotope value",
    colour = "Population"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
    legend.position = "none",
    
    # Rotate vertical strip text
    strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 15),  # 0 = horizontal, 90 = vertical
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-2.5, 2.5))

# Convert your table to an sf object
locations_sf <- dat_plot %>%
  dplyr::select(site, lon, lat) %>%
  distinct() %>% 
  st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)  # WGS84

# fix point longitudes to -180..180
locations_fixed <- dat_plot %>%
  dplyr::select(site, lon, lat) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(
    lon = as.numeric(lon),
    lon = ifelse(lon > 180, lon - 360, lon)      # wrap >180 back into -180..180
  ) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)

# Get New Zealand map
nz <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", country = "New Zealand", returnclass = "sf")

library(ggrepel)

# Plot
nz_map <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = nz, fill = "grey95", color = "black") +
  geom_sf(data = locations_sf, aes(color = site), size = 3) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = locations_sf,
    aes(x = st_coordinates(geometry)[,1],
        y = st_coordinates(geometry)[,2],
        label = site),
    size = 3,
    nudge_y = 0.5,
    max.overlaps = 50
  ) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(165, 185), ylim = c(-51, -30)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(color = "Population", 
       title = "Sampling locations in New Zealand") +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(), 
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    title = element_blank()
  )

nz_map + scatterplot + vc_pie_chart +
  plot_layout(widths = c(2, 2, 1), heights = c(3, 1, 1))
```

```{r}
dat_ <- 
  CNOH_dat_meta_full %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  filter(Feather_type == "Breast") %>% 
  mutate(
    Specimen_ID = fct_reorder(as.factor(Specimen_ID), lon),
    Specific_collection_location = as.factor(Specific_collection_location),
    isotope = as.character(isotope),
    mean_value = as.numeric(mean_value)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_value)) %>%
  group_by(isotope) %>%
  mutate(value_scaled = as.numeric(scale(mean_value))) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  group_by(isotope, Specific_collection_location) %>% 
  mutate(n_inds = n_distinct(Specimen_ID)) #%>% 
  # filter(n_inds > 1)

dat_ %>% 
  filter(geo_region == "New Zealand" & isotope == "normalised_d2h") %>% 
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(aes(x = lon, y = value_scaled))

# --- Fit lmer models per isotope --------------------------------------------
lmer_results <- list()
vc_table <- tibble()
icc_table <- tibble()
isotopes <- unique(dat$isotope)

for (iso in isotopes) {
message("Fitting lmer for ", iso)
d_iso <- dat_ %>% filter(isotope == iso)


# Ensure there are repeated measures; if not, skip
if (n_distinct(d_iso$Specimen_ID) < 2) {
warning("Not enough individuals for isotope ", iso, " - skipping")
next
}


# Fit model
form <- as.formula("value_scaled ~ lat + lon + (1 | Specific_collection_location)")
m <- tryCatch(lmer(form, data = d_iso, REML = TRUE), error = function(e) e)
if (inherits(m, "error")) {
warning("lmer failed for ", iso, ": ", m$message)
next
}


lmer_results[[iso]] <- m
vc <- get_varcomp_lmer(m)$table %>% mutate(isotope = iso)
vc_table <- bind_rows(vc_table, vc)
icc <- calc_repeatability(m, group = "Specimen_ID")
icc_table <- bind_rows(icc_table, tibble(isotope = iso, repeatability = icc))
}


# Tidy vc_table for plotting: ensure four groups present per isotope
vc_plot_df <- vc_table %>%
mutate(group = factor(group, levels = unique(group))) %>%
rename(variance = variance, proportion = prop)

# Tidy vc_table for plotting: ensure four groups present per isotope
vc_plot_df <- vc_table %>%
mutate(group = factor(group, levels = unique(group))) %>%
rename(variance = variance, proportion = prop)


# --- Plot variance partitioning per isotope ---------------------------------
# Prepare a plot dataframe from lmer results (preferred for clarity)
plot_df <- vc_table %>%
rename(component = group) %>%
mutate(component = recode(component,
"Specific_collection_location" = "Population",
"Residual" = "Residual")) %>%
group_by(isotope) %>%
mutate(component = factor(component, levels = c("Population","Residual"))) %>%
ungroup()


# p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = isotope, y = prop, fill = component)) +
#   geom_col(position = "stack") +
#   scale_x_discrete(labels = iso_labels) +
#   labs(
#     x = "Isotope",
#     y = "Proportion of total variance",
#     fill = "Component",
#     title = "Variance partitioning per isotope"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1")
# p

vc_pie_chart <- 
  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = "", y = prop, fill = component)) +
    geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
    coord_polar(theta = "y") +
    facet_grid(isotope ~ ., labeller = labeller(isotope = iso_labels)) +
    labs(
        x = NULL,
        y = NULL,
        fill = "Variance\ncomponent\npartitioning",
        title = "Variance partitioning per isotope"
    ) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1",
    guide = guide_legend(
      title.position = "top",    # move title above keys
      title.hjust = 0.5,         # center title
      label.position = "right"   # labels stay to the right of keys
    )) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_blank(),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        title = element_blank(),
        
        # Legend customization
        legend.position = "right",        # move legend to the right
        legend.direction = "vertical",    # legend items stacked (default), see below
        # legend.text = element_text(angle = 90)
        # legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", angle = 90),
        # legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"),   # increase spacing between keys
        # legend.key.width  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    
    # Increase white gap between keys
    # legend.spacing.x = unit(5, "cm") 
    )

vc_pie_chart

# Reorder individuals by population
dat_plot <- dat_ %>%
  mutate(
    Specimen_ID = fct_reorder(Specimen_ID, lat),
    isotope = factor(isotope)
  )

# Create a named vector mapping original isotope names to formatted labels
iso_labels <- c(
  "normalised_d13c" = "delta^{13}*C",
  "normalised_d15n" = "delta^{15}*N",
  "normalised_d2h"  = "delta^{2}*H",
  "normalised_d18o" = "delta^{18}*O"
)

scatterplot <- 
  ggplot(dat_plot, aes(
    x = Specimen_ID,
    y = value_scaled,
    colour = Specific_collection_location
  )) +
  geom_jitter(
    size = 2.5,
    alpha = 0.8, width = 0.15
  ) +

  # # Mean ± 95% CI per individual
  # stat_summary(
  #   fun = mean,
  #   fun.min = function(z) mean(z) - 1.96 * sd(z) / sqrt(length(z)),
  #   fun.max = function(z) mean(z) + 1.96 * sd(z) / sqrt(length(z)),
  #   geom = "pointrange",
  #   colour = "black",
  #   size = 0.4,
  #   position = position_nudge(x = 0.15)  # CI nudged right
  # ) +

  facet_grid(isotope ~ ., scales = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(isotope = as_labeller(iso_labels, label_parsed))) +
  labs(
    x = "Individual",
    y = "Scaled isotope value",
    colour = "Population"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
    legend.position = "none",
    
    # Rotate vertical strip text
    strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 15),  # 0 = horizontal, 90 = vertical
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  geom_smooth()

scatterplot + vc_pie_chart

```


### Spatial d13C

```{r}
model_spat_C <- lmer(value ~ lat + lon + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
                     data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "normalised_d13c"))
```

```{r}
spatial_effects <- allEffects(model_spat_C)
plot(spatial_effects)
```

```{r}
# Derive confidence intervals of effect sizes from parametric bootstrapping
tidy_spat_C <-
  tidy(model_spat_C, conf.int = TRUE, conf.method = "boot", nsim = 1000)

# Calculate marginal and conditional R-squared
r_squared <- r.squaredGLMM(model_spat_C)

# Display the results
print(r_squared)

# run rptR to obtain repeatabilities of random effects
rpt_spat_C <-
  rpt(value ~ lat + lon + (1 | Specimen_ID),
      grname = c("Specimen_ID", "Fixed"),
      data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "normalised_d13c"),
      datatype = "Gaussian",
      nboot = 1000, npermut = 1000, ratio = TRUE,
      adjusted = TRUE, ncores = 4, parallel = TRUE)

# run partR2 on each model to obtain marginal R2, parameter estimates, and beta
# weights
R2m_spat_C <-
  partR2(model_spat_C,
         partvars = c("lat",
                      "lon"),
         R2_type = "marginal",
         nboot = 1000,
         CI = 0.95,
         max_level = 1)

R2c_spat_C <-
  partR2(model_spat_C,
         partvars = c("lat",
                      "lon"),
         R2_type = "conditional",
         nboot = 1000,
         CI = 0.95,
         max_level = 1)

stats_spat_C <-
  list(mod = model_spat_C,
       tidy = tidy_spat_C,
       rptR = rpt_spat_C,
       partR2m = R2m_spat_C,
       partR2c = R2c_spat_C,
       data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "normalised_d13c"))

# summary(glht(smi_wing_lmer_seq))

#### Table of effect sizes ----
# Retrieve sample sizes
sample_sizes <-
  stats_spat_C$data %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  summarise(Individual = n_distinct(Specimen_ID),
            Observations = nrow(.))

sample_sizes <-
  as.data.frame(t(as.data.frame(sample_sizes))) %>%
  rownames_to_column("term") %>%
  rename(estimate = V1) %>%
  mutate(stat = "n")

# clean model component names
mod_comp_names <-
  data.frame(comp_name = c("baseline \U03B4 13 C (intercept)",
                           "latitude",
                           "longitude",
                           "Total Marginal \U1D479\U00B2",
                           "latitude",
                           "longitude",
                           "Total Conditional \U1D479\U00B2",
                           "Individual",
                           "Residual",
                           "Individual",
                           "Residual",
                           "Individuals",
                           "Observations"))

# Fixed effect sizes (non-standardized)
fixefTable <-
  stats_spat_C$tidy %>%
  dplyr::filter(effect == "fixed") %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "fixed")

# Fixed effect sizes (standardized)
fixef_bw_Table <-
  stats_spat_C$partR2m$BW %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "fixed_bw") %>%
  rename(conf.low = CI_lower,
         conf.high = CI_upper)

# Semi-partial R2 estimates
R2Table <-
  bind_rows(stats_spat_C$partR2m$R2,
            stats_spat_C$partR2c$R2[1,]) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, CI_lower, CI_upper) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "partR2") %>%
  rename(conf.low = CI_lower,
         conf.high = CI_upper)

# Random effects variances
ranefTable <-
  stats_spat_C$tidy %>%
  dplyr::filter(effect == "ran_pars") %>%
  dplyr::select(group, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "rand") %>%
  rename(term = group) %>%
  mutate(estimate = estimate^2,
         conf.high = conf.high^2,
         conf.low = conf.low^2)

# Adjusted repeatabilities
coefRptTable <-
  stats_spat_C$rptR$R_boot %>%
  dplyr::select(-Fixed) %>%
  mutate(residual = 1 - rowSums(.)) %>%
  apply(., 2,
        function(x) c(mean (x), quantile (x, prob = c(0.025, 0.975)))) %>%
  t() %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("term") %>%
  rename(estimate = V1,
         conf.low = `2.5%`,
         conf.high = `97.5%`) %>%
  mutate(stat = "RptR")

# Store all parameters into a single table and clean it up
allCoefs_mod <-
  bind_rows(fixefTable[1,], 
            fixef_bw_Table,
            R2Table,
            ranefTable,
            coefRptTable,
            sample_sizes) %>%
  bind_cols(.,
            mod_comp_names) %>%
  mutate(coefString = ifelse(!is.na(conf.low),
                             paste0("[",
                                    round(conf.low, 2), ", ",
                                    round(conf.high, 2), "]"),
                             NA),
         effect = c(rep("Fixed effects \U1D6FD (standardized)", nrow(fixefTable[1,])),
                    rep("Fixed effects \U1D6FD (standardized)", nrow(fixef_bw_Table)),
                    rep("Partitioned \U1D479\U00B2", nrow(R2Table)),
                    rep("Random effects \U1D70E\U00B2", nrow(ranefTable)),
                    rep("Adjusted repeatability \U1D45F", nrow(coefRptTable)),
                    rep("Sample sizes \U1D45B", nrow(sample_sizes)))) %>%
  dplyr::select(effect, everything())

# draw gt table
stats_spat_C_table <-
  allCoefs_mod %>%
  dplyr::select(effect, comp_name, estimate, coefString) %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "row",
     groupname_col = "effect") %>%
  cols_label(comp_name = html("<i>\U03B4 13 C</i>"),
             estimate = "Mean estimate",
             coefString = "95% confidence interval") %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate),
             rows = 1:11,
             decimals = 2,
             use_seps = FALSE) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate),
             rows = 12:13,
             decimals = 0,
             use_seps = FALSE) %>%
  sub_missing(columns = 1:4,
              missing_text = "") %>%
  cols_align(align = "left",
             columns = c(comp_name)) %>%
  tab_options(row_group.font.weight = "bold",
              row_group.background.color = brewer.pal(9,"Greys")[3],
              table.font.size = 12,
              data_row.padding = 3,
              row_group.padding = 4,
              summary_row.padding = 2,
              column_labels.font.size = 14,
              row_group.font.size = 12,
              table.width = pct(90))
```

```{r}
stats_spat_C_table
```

### Spatial d15N

```{r}
model_spat_N <- lmer(value ~ lat + lon + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
                     data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "normalised_d15n"))
```

```{r}
spatial_effects <- allEffects(model_spat_N)
plot(spatial_effects)
```

```{r}
# Derive confidence intervals of effect sizes from parametric bootstrapping
tidy_spat_N<-
  tidy(model_spat_N, conf.int = TRUE, conf.method = "boot", nsim = 1000)

# run rptR to obtain repeatabilities of random effects
rpt_spat_N <-
  rpt(value ~ lat + lon + (1 | Specimen_ID),
      grname = c("Specimen_ID", "Fixed"),
      data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "normalised_d15n"),
      datatype = "Gaussian",
      nboot = 1000, npermut = 1000, ratio = TRUE,
      adjusted = TRUE, ncores = 4, parallel = TRUE)

# run partR2 on each model to obtain marginal R2, parameter estimates, and beta
# weights
R2m_spat_N <-
  partR2(model_spat_N,
         partvars = c("lat",
                      "lon"),
         R2_type = "marginal",
         nboot = 1000,
         CI = 0.95,
         max_level = 1)

R2c_spat_N <-
  partR2(model_spat_N,
         partvars = c("lat",
                      "lon"),
         R2_type = "conditional",
         nboot = 1000,
         CI = 0.95,
         max_level = 1)

stats_spat_N <-
  list(mod = model_spat_N,
       tidy = tidy_spat_N,
       rptR = rpt_spat_N,
       partR2m = R2m_spat_N,
       partR2c = R2c_spat_N,
       data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "normalised_d15n"))

# summary(glht(model_spat_N))

#### Table of effect sizes ----
# Retrieve sample sizes
sample_sizes <-
  stats_spat_N$data %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  summarise(Individual = n_distinct(Specimen_ID),
            Observations = nrow(.))

sample_sizes <-
  as.data.frame(t(as.data.frame(sample_sizes))) %>%
  rownames_to_column("term") %>%
  rename(estimate = V1) %>%
  mutate(stat = "n")

# clean model component names
mod_comp_names <-
  data.frame(comp_name = c("baseline \U03B4 15 N (intercept)",
                           "latitude",
                           "longitude",
                           "Total Marginal \U1D479\U00B2",
                           "latitude",
                           "longitude",
                           "Total Conditional \U1D479\U00B2",
                           "Individual",
                           "Residual",
                           "Individual",
                           "Residual",
                           "Individuals",
                           "Observations"))

# Fixed effect sizes (non-standardized)
fixefTable <-
  stats_spat_N$tidy %>%
  dplyr::filter(effect == "fixed") %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "fixed")

# Fixed effect sizes (standardized)
fixef_bw_Table <-
  stats_spat_N$partR2m$BW %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "fixed_bw") %>%
  rename(conf.low = CI_lower,
         conf.high = CI_upper)

# Semi-partial R2 estimates
R2Table <-
  bind_rows(stats_spat_N$partR2m$R2,
            stats_spat_N$partR2c$R2[1,]) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, CI_lower, CI_upper) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "partR2") %>%
  rename(conf.low = CI_lower,
         conf.high = CI_upper)

# Random effects variances
ranefTable <-
  stats_spat_N$tidy %>%
  dplyr::filter(effect == "ran_pars") %>%
  dplyr::select(group, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "rand") %>%
  rename(term = group) %>%
  mutate(estimate = estimate^2,
         conf.high = conf.high^2,
         conf.low = conf.low^2)

# Adjusted repeatabilities
coefRptTable <-
  stats_spat_N$rptR$R_boot %>%
  dplyr::select(-Fixed) %>%
  mutate(residual = 1 - rowSums(.)) %>%
  apply(., 2,
        function(x) c(mean (x), quantile (x, prob = c(0.025, 0.975)))) %>%
  t() %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("term") %>%
  rename(estimate = V1,
         conf.low = `2.5%`,
         conf.high = `97.5%`) %>%
  mutate(stat = "RptR")

# Store all parameters into a single table and clean it up
allCoefs_mod <-
  bind_rows(fixefTable[1,], 
            fixef_bw_Table,
            R2Table,
            ranefTable,
            coefRptTable,
            sample_sizes) %>%
  bind_cols(.,
            mod_comp_names) %>%
  mutate(coefString = ifelse(!is.na(conf.low),
                             paste0("[",
                                    round(conf.low, 2), ", ",
                                    round(conf.high, 2), "]"),
                             NA),
         effect = c(rep("Fixed effects \U1D6FD (standardized)", nrow(fixefTable[1,])),
                    rep("Fixed effects \U1D6FD (standardized)", nrow(fixef_bw_Table)),
                    rep("Partitioned \U1D479\U00B2", nrow(R2Table)),
                    rep("Random effects \U1D70E\U00B2", nrow(ranefTable)),
                    rep("Adjusted repeatability \U1D45F", nrow(coefRptTable)),
                    rep("Sample sizes \U1D45B", nrow(sample_sizes)))) %>%
  dplyr::select(effect, everything())

# draw gt table
stats_spat_N_table <-
  allCoefs_mod %>%
  dplyr::select(effect, comp_name, estimate, coefString) %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "row",
     groupname_col = "effect") %>%
  cols_label(comp_name = html("<i>\U03B4 15 N</i>"),
             estimate = "Mean estimate",
             coefString = "95% confidence interval") %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate),
             rows = 1:11,
             decimals = 2,
             use_seps = FALSE) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate),
             rows = 12:13,
             decimals = 0,
             use_seps = FALSE) %>%
  sub_missing(columns = 1:4,
              missing_text = "") %>%
  cols_align(align = "left",
             columns = c(comp_name)) %>%
  tab_options(row_group.font.weight = "bold",
              row_group.background.color = brewer.pal(9,"Greys")[3],
              table.font.size = 12,
              data_row.padding = 3,
              row_group.padding = 4,
              summary_row.padding = 2,
              column_labels.font.size = 14,
              row_group.font.size = 12,
              table.width = pct(90))
```

```{r}
stats_spat_N_table
```

### Spatial C:N

```{r, include=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
model_spat_CN <- lmer(value ~ lat + lon + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
                      data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "cn_mass_ratio") %>% filter(!is.na(value) & !is.na(lat) & !is.na(lon) & !is.na(Specimen_ID)))

spatial_effects <- allEffects(model_spat_CN)
plot(spatial_effects)

# Derive confidence intervals of effect sizes from parametric bootstrapping
tidy_spat_CN<-
  tidy(model_spat_CN, conf.int = TRUE, conf.method = "boot", nsim = 1000)

# run rptR to obtain repeatabilities of random effects
rpt_spat_CN <-
  rpt(value ~ lat + lon + (1 | Specimen_ID),
      grname = c("Specimen_ID", "Fixed"),
      data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "cn_mass_ratio") %>% filter(!is.na(value)),
      datatype = "Gaussian",
      nboot = 1000, npermut = 1000, ratio = TRUE,
      adjusted = TRUE, ncores = 4, parallel = TRUE)

# run partR2 on each model to obtain marginal R2, parameter estimates, and beta
# weights
R2m_spat_CN <-
  partR2(model_spat_CN,
         partvars = c("lat",
                      "lon"),
         R2_type = "marginal",
         nboot = 1000,
         CI = 0.95,
         max_level = 1)

R2c_spat_CN <-
  partR2(model_spat_CN,
         partvars = c("lat",
                      "lon"),
         R2_type = "conditional",
         nboot = 1000,
         CI = 0.95,
         max_level = 1)

stats_spat_CN <-
  list(mod = model_spat_CN,
       tidy = tidy_spat_CN,
       rptR = rpt_spat_CN,
       partR2m = R2m_spat_CN,
       partR2c = R2c_spat_CN,
       data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "cn_mass_ratio") %>% filter(!is.na(value)))

# summary(glht(model_spat_CN))

#### Table of effect sizes ----
# Retrieve sample sizes
sample_sizes <-
  stats_spat_CN$data %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  summarise(Individual = n_distinct(Specimen_ID),
            Observations = nrow(.))

sample_sizes <-
  as.data.frame(t(as.data.frame(sample_sizes))) %>%
  rownames_to_column("term") %>%
  rename(estimate = V1) %>%
  mutate(stat = "n")

# clean model component names
mod_comp_names <-
  data.frame(comp_name = c("baseline \U03B4 13 C : \U03B4 15 N (intercept)",
                           "latitude",
                           "longitude",
                           "Total Marginal \U1D479\U00B2",
                           "latitude",
                           "longitude",
                           "Total Conditional \U1D479\U00B2",
                           "Individual",
                           "Residual",
                           "Individual",
                           "Residual",
                           "Individuals",
                           "Observations"))

# Fixed effect sizes (non-standardized)
fixefTable <-
  stats_spat_CN$tidy %>%
  dplyr::filter(effect == "fixed") %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "fixed")

# Fixed effect sizes (standardized)
fixef_bw_Table <-
  stats_spat_CN$partR2m$BW %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "fixed_bw") %>%
  rename(conf.low = CI_lower,
         conf.high = CI_upper)

# Semi-partial R2 estimates
R2Table <-
  bind_rows(stats_spat_CN$partR2m$R2,
            stats_spat_CN$partR2c$R2[1,]) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, CI_lower, CI_upper) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "partR2") %>%
  rename(conf.low = CI_lower,
         conf.high = CI_upper)

# Random effects variances
ranefTable <-
  stats_spat_CN$tidy %>%
  dplyr::filter(effect == "ran_pars") %>%
  dplyr::select(group, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "rand") %>%
  rename(term = group) %>%
  mutate(estimate = estimate^2,
         conf.high = conf.high^2,
         conf.low = conf.low^2)

# Adjusted repeatabilities
coefRptTable <-
  stats_spat_CN$rptR$R_boot %>%
  dplyr::select(-Fixed) %>%
  mutate(residual = 1 - rowSums(.)) %>%
  apply(., 2,
        function(x) c(mean (x), quantile (x, prob = c(0.025, 0.975)))) %>%
  t() %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("term") %>%
  rename(estimate = V1,
         conf.low = `2.5%`,
         conf.high = `97.5%`) %>%
  mutate(stat = "RptR")

# Store all parameters into a single table and clean it up
allCoefs_mod <-
  bind_rows(fixefTable[1,], 
            fixef_bw_Table,
            R2Table,
            ranefTable,
            coefRptTable,
            sample_sizes) %>%
  bind_cols(.,
            mod_comp_names) %>%
  mutate(coefString = ifelse(!is.na(conf.low),
                             paste0("[",
                                    round(conf.low, 2), ", ",
                                    round(conf.high, 2), "]"),
                             NA),
         effect = c(rep("Fixed effects \U1D6FD (standardized)", nrow(fixefTable[1,])),
                    rep("Fixed effects \U1D6FD (standardized)", nrow(fixef_bw_Table)),
                    rep("Partitioned \U1D479\U00B2", nrow(R2Table)),
                    rep("Random effects \U1D70E\U00B2", nrow(ranefTable)),
                    rep("Adjusted repeatability \U1D45F", nrow(coefRptTable)),
                    rep("Sample sizes \U1D45B", nrow(sample_sizes)))) %>%
  dplyr::select(effect, everything())

# draw gt table
stats_spat_CN_table <-
  allCoefs_mod %>%
  dplyr::select(effect, comp_name, estimate, coefString) %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "row",
     groupname_col = "effect") %>%
  cols_label(comp_name = html("<i>\U03B4 13 C : \U03B4 15 N</i>"),
             estimate = "Mean estimate",
             coefString = "95% confidence interval") %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate),
             rows = 1:11,
             decimals = 2,
             use_seps = FALSE) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate),
             rows = 12:13,
             decimals = 0,
             use_seps = FALSE) %>%
  sub_missing(columns = 1:4,
              missing_text = "") %>%
  cols_align(align = "left",
             columns = c(comp_name)) %>%
  tab_options(row_group.font.weight = "bold",
              row_group.background.color = brewer.pal(9,"Greys")[3],
              table.font.size = 12,
              data_row.padding = 3,
              row_group.padding = 4,
              summary_row.padding = 2,
              column_labels.font.size = 14,
              row_group.font.size = 12,
              table.width = pct(90))

stats_spat_CN_table
```

### Spatial d18O

```{r}
model_spat_O <- lmer(value ~ lat + lon + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
                     data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "normalised_d18o"),
                     control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5)))
```

```{r}
spatial_effects <- allEffects(model_spat_O)
plot(spatial_effects)
```

```{r}
# Derive confidence intervals of effect sizes from parametric bootstrapping
tidy_spat_O <-
  tidy(model_spat_O, conf.int = TRUE, conf.method = "boot", nsim = 1000)

# run rptR to obtain repeatabilities of random effects
rpt_spat_O <-
  rpt(value ~ lat + lon + (1 | Specimen_ID),
      grname = c("Specimen_ID", "Fixed"),
      data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "normalised_d18o"),
      datatype = "Gaussian",
      nboot = 1000, npermut = 1000, ratio = TRUE,
      adjusted = TRUE, ncores = 4, parallel = TRUE)

# run partR2 on each model to obtain marginal R2, parameter estimates, and beta
# weights
R2m_spat_O <-
  partR2(model_spat_O,
         partvars = c("lat",
                      "lon"),
         R2_type = "marginal",
         nboot = 1000,
         CI = 0.95,
         max_level = 1)

R2c_spat_O <-
  partR2(model_spat_O,
         partvars = c("lat",
                      "lon"),
         R2_type = "conditional",
         nboot = 1000,
         CI = 0.95,
         max_level = 1)

stats_spat_O <-
  list(mod = model_spat_O,
       tidy = tidy_spat_O,
       rptR = rpt_spat_O,
       partR2m = R2m_spat_O,
       partR2c = R2c_spat_O,
       data = triplicate_CNOH_dat_meta %>% filter(isotope == "normalised_d18o"))

# summary(glht(model_spat_N))

#### Table of effect sizes ----
# Retrieve sample sizes
sample_sizes <-
  stats_spat_O$data %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  summarise(Individual = n_distinct(Specimen_ID),
            Observations = nrow(.))

sample_sizes <-
  as.data.frame(t(as.data.frame(sample_sizes))) %>%
  rownames_to_column("term") %>%
  rename(estimate = V1) %>%
  mutate(stat = "n")

# clean model component names
mod_comp_names <-
  data.frame(comp_name = c("baseline \U03B4 18 O (intercept)",
                           "latitude",
                           "longitude",
                           "Total Marginal \U1D479\U00B2",
                           "latitude",
                           "longitude",
                           "Total Conditional \U1D479\U00B2",
                           "Individual",
                           "Residual",
                           "Individual",
                           "Residual",
                           "Individuals",
                           "Observations"))

# Fixed effect sizes (non-standardized)
fixefTable <-
  stats_spat_O$tidy %>%
  dplyr::filter(effect == "fixed") %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "fixed")

# Fixed effect sizes (standardized)
fixef_bw_Table <-
  stats_spat_O$partR2m$BW %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "fixed_bw") %>%
  rename(conf.low = CI_lower,
         conf.high = CI_upper)

# Semi-partial R2 estimates
R2Table <-
  bind_rows(stats_spat_O$partR2m$R2,
            stats_spat_O$partR2c$R2[1,]) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, CI_lower, CI_upper) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "partR2") %>%
  rename(conf.low = CI_lower,
         conf.high = CI_upper)

# Random effects variances
ranefTable <-
  stats_spat_O$tidy %>%
  dplyr::filter(effect == "ran_pars") %>%
  dplyr::select(group, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(stat = "rand") %>%
  rename(term = group) %>%
  mutate(estimate = estimate^2,
         conf.high = conf.high^2,
         conf.low = conf.low^2)

# Adjusted repeatabilities
coefRptTable <-
  stats_spat_O$rptR$R_boot %>%
  dplyr::select(-Fixed) %>%
  mutate(residual = 1 - rowSums(.)) %>%
  apply(., 2,
        function(x) c(mean (x), quantile (x, prob = c(0.025, 0.975)))) %>%
  t() %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("term") %>%
  rename(estimate = V1,
         conf.low = `2.5%`,
         conf.high = `97.5%`) %>%
  mutate(stat = "RptR")

# Store all parameters into a single table and clean it up
allCoefs_mod <-
  bind_rows(fixefTable[1,], 
            fixef_bw_Table,
            R2Table,
            ranefTable,
            coefRptTable,
            sample_sizes) %>%
  bind_cols(.,
            mod_comp_names) %>%
  mutate(coefString = ifelse(!is.na(conf.low),
                             paste0("[",
                                    round(conf.low, 2), ", ",
                                    round(conf.high, 2), "]"),
                             NA),
         effect = c(rep("Fixed effects \U1D6FD (standardized)", nrow(fixefTable[1,])),
                    rep("Fixed effects \U1D6FD (standardized)", nrow(fixef_bw_Table)),
                    rep("Partitioned \U1D479\U00B2", nrow(R2Table)),
                    rep("Random effects \U1D70E\U00B2", nrow(ranefTable)),
                    rep("Adjusted repeatability \U1D45F", nrow(coefRptTable)),
                    rep("Sample sizes \U1D45B", nrow(sample_sizes)))) %>%
  dplyr::select(effect, everything())

# draw gt table
stats_spat_O_table <-
  allCoefs_mod %>%
  dplyr::select(effect, comp_name, estimate, coefString) %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "row",
     groupname_col = "effect") %>%
  cols_label(comp_name = html("<i>\U03B4 18 O</i>"),
             estimate = "Mean estimate",
             coefString = "95% confidence interval") %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate),
             rows = 1:11,
             decimals = 2,
             use_seps = FALSE) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate),
             rows = 12:13,
             decimals = 0,
             use_seps = FALSE) %>%
  sub_missing(columns = 1:4,
              missing_text = "") %>%
  cols_align(align = "left",
             columns = c(comp_name)) %>%
  tab_options(row_group.font.weight = "bold",
              row_group.background.color = brewer.pal(9,"Greys")[3],
              table.font.size = 12,
              data_row.padding = 3,
              row_group.padding = 4,
              summary_row.padding = 2,
              column_labels.font.size = 14,
              row_group.font.size = 12,
              table.width = pct(90))
```

```{r}
stats_spat_O_table
```

## Different sampling and homogenization proceedures
half a vane vs. sub-sample of scissor cut vane homogenate

```{r}
# import and consolidate key columns
dat <-
  read_excel(here("data/Master_SI_Replicate test Feathers_N_9_LEH_Jan25_v1_SB.xls"), 
                     sheet = "Sample results_C&N", 
                     col_types = "text", skip = 9, col_names = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(1, 2, 10:16, 18:24, 28:35, 37, 40:46, 48) %>%
    rename_with(~ .x %>%
                tolower() %>%
                str_replace_all(" ", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("__", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("___", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("%", "_perc_") %>%
                str_replace_all(":", "") %>%
                str_replace_all("#", "")) %>% 
  rename(all_correct_amt_perc__n_flash_tcd_____A = all_correct_amt_perc__n_flash_tcd...10, 
         `ampl_28_mv_____A` = `ampl_28_(mv)...11`, 
         normalised_d15n_____A = normalised_d15n...12,                
         avg_amt_perc_n_____A = avg_amt_perc_n...13,             
         stdev_amt_perc_n_____A = stdev_amt_perc_n...14,                  
         avg_d15n_____A = avg_d15n...15,    
         stdev_d15n_____A = stdev_d15n...16,                        
         all_correct_amt_perc__n_flash_tcd_____B = all_correct_amt_perc__n_flash_tcd...18,
         `ampl_28_mv_____B` = `ampl_28_(mv)...19`,                     
         normalised_d15n_____B = normalised_d15n...20,    
         avg_amt_perc_n_____B = avg_amt_perc_n...21,                    
         stdev_amt_perc_n_____B = stdev_amt_perc_n...22,  
         avg_d15n_____B = avg_d15n...23,                          
         stdev_d15n_____B = stdev_d15n...24,   
         all_correct_amt_perc__c_flash_tcd_____C = all_correct_amt_perc__c_flash_tcd...28,
         ampl_44_mv_____C = `ampl_44_(mv)...29`,                      
         normalised_d13c_____C = normalised_d13c...30, 
         sample_dilution__perc_____C = `sample_dilution_(_perc_)`,               
         avg_amt_perc_c_____C = avg_amt_perc_c...32,   
         stdev_amt_perc_c_____C = stdev_amt_perc_c...33,                       
         avg_d13c_____C = avg_d13c...34, 
         stdev_d13c_____C = stdev_d13c...35,                             
         cn_mass_ratio_____C = `cn_mass_ratio,_second_batch`,            
         all_correct_amt_perc__c_flash_tcd_____D = all_correct_amt_perc__c_flash_tcd...40,
         ampl_44_mv_____D = `ampl_44_(mv)...41`,                      
         normalised_d13c_____D = normalised_d13c...42, 
         avg_amt_perc_C_____D = avg_amt_perc_c...43,                    
         stdev_amt_perc_C_____D = stdev_amt_perc_c...44,                 
         avg_d13C_____D = avg_d13c...45,                          
         stdev_d13C_____D = stdev_d13c...46,                       
         cn_mass_ratio_____D = `cn_mass_ratio,_first_batch`) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(index_, identifier_1), 
    names_to = c("Group", "Variable"), 
    names_sep = "_____"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(method = ifelse(Variable %in% c("A", "C"), "B", "A"),
         element = ifelse(Variable %in% c("A", "B"), "N", "C"),
         value = as.numeric(value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    NewColumn = str_extract(identifier_1, "^[^ ]+"),  # Extract text to the left of the first white space
    feather_id = str_trim(str_replace(identifier_1, "^[^ ]+ ", "")),
    identifier_1 = str_trim(str_replace(identifier_1, "^[^ ]+ ", ""))# Remove text to the left of the first white space and trim remaining white space
  ) %>% mutate(identifier_1 = str_replace(identifier_1, "_.*", "")) %>% 
  rename(Specimen_ID = NewColumn,
         Feather_type = identifier_1) %>% 
  left_join(., read_excel(here("../../stable_isotopes/data/for_Sarah/master_museum_sample_banded_dotterels.xlsx"), col_types = "text") %>% mutate(Specimen_ID = str_extract(Specimen_ID, "^[^ ]+")) %>% dplyr::select(Feather_type, Specimen_ID, General_collection_location, 
         Specific_collection_location, Collection_date), join_by(Specimen_ID, Feather_type)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(Specimen_ID, feather_id, Feather_type, Group, method, 
         element, General_collection_location, 
         Specific_collection_location, Collection_date, value) %>%
  mutate(Month = str_extract(Collection_date, "(January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|September|October|November|December)")) %>% 
  mutate(location = paste(Specific_collection_location, General_collection_location, sep = ", ")) %>%
  mutate(location = str_replace_all(location, "NA, ", ""))

dat_C <-
  dat %>% filter(element == "C") %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = value)

dat_N <-  
  dat %>% filter(element == "N") %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = Group, values_from = value)
  
# Create a data frame 
df <- data.frame(location = dat$location)

# Remove duplicates to avoid redundant API calls
unique_locations <- unique(df$location)

# Get latitude and longitude for unique locations
geocode_data <- geocode(unique_locations)

# Combine the geocode data with the unique locations
location_data <- data.frame(location = unique_locations, geocode_data)

# Merge the geocode data back into the original dataframe
dat_C <- left_join(dat_C, location_data, by = "location")
dat_N <- left_join(dat_N, location_data, by = "location")

# transform lon
dat_C <-
  dat_C %>% 
  mutate(lon = ifelse(lon < 0, lon + 180*2, lon))

dat_N <-
  dat_N %>% 
  mutate(lon = ifelse(lon < 0, lon + 180*2, lon))

dat_C_mean <-
  dat_C %>%
  group_by(Specimen_ID, method) %>%
  summarise(normalised_d13c = mean(normalised_d13c))

dat_N_mean <-
  dat_N %>%
  group_by(Specimen_ID, method) %>%
  summarise(normalised_d15n = mean(normalised_d15n))

dat_NC_mean <- 
  left_join(dat_C_mean, dat_N_mean)

dat_N_ <- 
  dat_N %>% 
  dplyr::select(method, Specimen_ID, feather_id, normalised_d15n)

dat_C_ <- 
  dat_C %>% 
  dplyr::select(method, Specimen_ID, feather_id, normalised_d13c)

dat_NC_2 <- 
  left_join(dat_C_, dat_N_)

# Compute convex hulls for each Specimen_ID using chull() from sp
hulls_ <- dat_NC_2 %>%
  group_by(method, Specimen_ID) %>%
  do({
    # Extract the data for the current group
    group_data <- .[ , c("normalised_d13c", "normalised_d15n")]
    
    # Create a spatial object but extract coordinates as a regular matrix
    coords <- SpatialPoints(group_data)
    coords_matrix <- coords@coords
    
    # Compute the convex hull using chull
    chull_indices <- chull(coords_matrix)
    hull_coords <- group_data[chull_indices, ]
    
    # Return the convex hull coordinates
    data.frame(hull = I(list(hull_coords)))
  }) %>%
  unnest(cols = c(hull)) %>% 
  left_join(., dat %>% dplyr::select(Specimen_ID, Month, location) %>% distinct(), by = "Specimen_ID") %>% 
  mutate(Specimen_ID_method = paste(Specimen_ID, method, sep = "_"))

ggplot() + 
  geom_polygon(data = hulls_, 
               aes(group = Specimen_ID_method, fill = location, 
                   x = normalised_d13c, y = normalised_d15n), 
               color = NA, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(data = hulls_, 
             aes(fill = location, x = normalised_d13c, y = normalised_d15n, shape = method), 
             size = 3) + 
  labs(x = "d13C", y = "d15N") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 24), labels = c("A" = "homogenate of entire half vane",
                                                    "B" = "homogenate of vane sub-sample")) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 15)) + 
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 3), fill = guide_legend(nrow = 3), shape = guide_legend(nrow = 3)) +
  geom_point(data = dat_NC_mean, aes(x = normalised_d13c, y = normalised_d15n, shape = method), fill = "black")
```

## assess quantitative differences in the isotope data according to sampling/homogenization method

```{r, eval=FALSE}
C_amt_perc_mod <- 
  lmer(all_correct_amt_perc__c_flash_tcd ~ method + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
       data = dat_C)

double_model = bf(all_correct_amt_perc__c_flash_tcd ~ method + gr(Specimen_ID, by = method),
                  sigma ~ method)

m3_brm <- brm(double_model,
              data = dat_C,
              warmup = 500,iter = 3000, thin=2,
              chains = 2, inits = "random",
              seed = 12345)


C_ampl_44_mv_mod <- 
  lmer(ampl_44_mv ~ method + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
       data = dat_C)

C_normalised_d13c_mod <- 
  lmer(normalised_d13c ~ method + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
       data = dat_C)

N_amt_perc_mod <- 
  lmer(all_correct_amt_perc__n_flash_tcd ~ method + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
       data = dat_N)

N_ampl_28_mv_mod <- 
  lmer(ampl_28_mv ~ method + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
       data = dat_N)

N_normalised_d15n_mod <- 
  lmer(normalised_d15n ~ method + (1 | Specimen_ID), 
       data = dat_N)
```

## Sample management

```{r}
museum_samples <- 
  read_sheet("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-iF-rvswS5QvlKgz4fgF14FnfkK17DYf6je5nwYw48o/edit?gid=0#gid=0", col_types = "c") %>% 
  filter(Sent_to_Sarah == "yes" | Sent_to_Sarah == "Yes") %>%
  mutate(Month = str_extract(Collection_date, "(January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|September|October|November|December)")) %>% 
  mutate(location = paste(gsub("\\s*\\([^\\)]+\\)", "", Specific_collection_location), General_collection_location, sep = ", ")) %>%
  mutate(location = str_replace_all(location, "NA, ", ""))

# Applying the transformation
museum_samples <- museum_samples %>%
  mutate(
    month_extracted = sapply(Collection_date, extract_month),
    month = ifelse(!is.na(Collection_month), as.numeric(Collection_month), month_extracted)
  ) %>%
  select(-month_extracted)

# Create a data frame 
museum_sample_loc <- 
  data.frame(location = museum_samples$location) %>% 
  pull(location) %>% unique()

geocode_data <- geocode(museum_sample_loc)

# Combine the geocode data with the unique locations
museum_sample_loc_ <- data.frame(location = museum_sample_loc, geocode_data)

# Merge the geocode data back into the original dataframe
museum_samples <- left_join(museum_samples, museum_sample_loc_, by = "location")

# Remove rows where month is NA
museum_samples <- museum_samples %>% filter(!is.na(month))

# Convert numeric month to month names
museum_samples$month <- factor(month.name[museum_samples$month], levels = month.name)

# Convert museum_samples to an sf object
museum_samples_sf <- st_as_sf(museum_samples, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)

# Reproject museum_samples to the desired projection
museum_samples_sf <- st_transform(museum_samples_sf, crs = "+proj=ortho +lon_0=160 +lat_0=-41")

museum_samples_sf <- 
  museum_samples_sf %>%
  mutate(Specimen_ID_ = gsub("WAM |AJB ", "", Specimen_ID)) %>%
  mutate(Feather_type = ifelse(is.na(Feather_type), sub("^(\\S+\\s+\\S+)\\s+(.*)$", "\\2", Specimen_ID), Feather_type)) %>% 
  mutate(
    Specimen_ID_ = str_extract(Specimen_ID_, "^[^ ]+")) %>%   # Extract text to the left of the first white space
  group_by(Specimen_ID_) %>%
  mutate(Feather_status = case_when(
    n_distinct(Feather_type) == 2 ~ "Primary and Breast",
    "Breast" %in% Feather_type ~ "Breast",
    "Primary" %in% Feather_type ~ "Primary"
  )) %>%
  ungroup()

museum_samples_sf %>% dplyr::select(Specimen_ID_, Feather_status) %>% distinct()

# Get world map data
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Extract the coordinates from the sf object
museum_samples_df <- 
  st_as_sf(museum_samples_sf %>% 
             dplyr::select(Museum, Specimen_ID_, Sex, location, Feather_status, month) %>% distinct()) %>%
  st_coordinates() %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  bind_cols(st_drop_geometry(museum_samples_sf %>% 
             dplyr::select(Museum, Specimen_ID_, Sex, location, Feather_status, month) %>% distinct()))

# Create the plot with projection centered on 180° longitude and custom bounds
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, fill = "lightgrey") +  # Add world map
  geom_point(data = museum_samples_df %>% 
               filter(month %in% c("May", "June", "July", "August")) %>% 
               mutate(geo_region = ifelse(str_detect(location, "Zealand"), "NZ", "AU")), 
             aes(x = X, y = Y, fill = geo_region),
             shape = 21, 
             size = 3, 
             alpha = 0.8, 
             position = position_jitter(width = 50000, height = 50000)) +  # Add jitter to points
  # facet_wrap(~ month, ncol = 3) +  # Create facets for each month
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-2312396, 1837687), ylim = c(-866762, 885204), 
           crs = "+proj=ortho +lon_0=160 +lat_0=-41") +  # Custom bounds for x and y axes
  theme_minimal() +  # Use a minimal theme
  labs(title = "Museum specimens collected in winter") +#, 
       # subtitle = "Collection Locations by Month", 
       # x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "grey40"),
        axis.title = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("AU" = "#E69F00", "NZ" = "#56B4E9"))

museum_samples_df %>% 
  filter(str_detect(location, "Australia") | 
           (str_detect(location, "New Zealand") & 
              month %in% c("May", "June", "July"))) %>% 
  group_by(location, X, Y, month) %>%
  mutate(n_ind_at_location = n_distinct(Specimen_ID_)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::select(-X, -Y) %>% 
  # filter(n_ind_at_location > 2) %>% View()
  arrange(desc(n_ind_at_location)) %>% 
  write.csv("data/museum_samples_to_analyze_161224.csv", row.names = FALSE)

museum_samples_df %>% 
  filter(str_detect(location, "Australia") | 
           (str_detect(location, "New Zealand") & 
              month %in% c("May", "June", "July"))) %>% 
  group_by(location, X, Y, month) %>%
  mutate(n_ind_at_location = n_distinct(Specimen_ID_)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::select(-X, -Y) %>%
  nrow()
```

```{r}
museum_samples %>% 
  group_by(Museum) %>% 
  summarise(n_museums = n_distinct(Specimen_ID)) %>%
  bind_rows(data.frame(Museum = "Total", 
                       n_museums = museum_samples %>% 
                         summarise(x = n_distinct(Specimen_ID)) %>% 
                         pull(x))) %>% 
  gt() %>% 
  cols_label(Museum = "Museum",
             n_museums = "No. specimens") %>% 
  tab_options(row_group.font.weight = "bold",
              row_group.background.color = brewer.pal(9,"Greys")[3],
              table.font.size = 12,
              data_row.padding = 3,
              row_group.padding = 4,
              summary_row.padding = 2,
              column_labels.font.size = 14,
              row_group.font.size = 12,
              table.width = pct(90))
```

## checking feather barbs vs. feather shaft

```{r}
dat <-
  read_excel(here("data/Master_EA_C_N_SI_Batch 3 Breast feathers_shaft and barb comparison_LEH_Sept25_v1_SB.xls"), 
                     sheet = "Sample results_C&N", 
                     col_types = "text", skip = 9, col_names = TRUE) %>%
    rename_with(~ .x %>%
                tolower() %>%
                str_replace_all(" ", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("__", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("___", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("%", "_perc_") %>%
                str_replace_all(":", "") %>%
                str_replace_all("#", "")) %>%
  rename(all_correct_amt_perc__n_flash_tcd = all_correct_amt_perc__n_flash_tcd, 
         `ampl_28_mv` = `ampl_28_(mv)`, 
         normalised_d15n = normalised_d15n,                
         avg_amt_perc_n = avg_amt_perc__n,             
         stdev_amt_perc_n = stdev_amt_perc__n,                  
         avg_d15n = avg_d15n,    
         stdev_d15n = stdev_d15n,                        
         all_correct_amt_perc__c_flash_tcd = all_correct_amt_perc__c_flash_tcd,
         ampl_44_mv = `ampl_44_(mv)`,
         normalised_d13c = normalised_d13c,
         sample_dilution__perc = `sample_dilution_(_perc_)`,
         avg_amt_perc_c = avg_amt_perc__c,
         stdev_amt_perc_c = stdev_amt_perc__c,
         avg_d13c = avg_d13c,
         stdev_d13c = stdev_d13c,
         cn_mass_ratio = `cn_mass_ratio`) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(index_)) %>%
  mutate(
    Feather_type = str_extract(identifier_1, regex("Primary|Breast", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    region       = str_extract(identifier_1, regex("Barb|Shaft", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    Specimen_ID  = str_extract(identifier_1, "^[^_]+")
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::select(Specimen_ID, Feather_type, region, c(4,9:35)) %>% 
  left_join(., read_excel(here("../../stable_isotopes/data/for_Sarah/master_museum_sample_banded_dotterels.xlsx"), col_types = "text") %>% mutate(Specimen_ID = str_extract(Specimen_ID, "^[^ ]+")) %>% dplyr::select(Feather_type, Specimen_ID, General_collection_location, 
         Specific_collection_location, Collection_date), join_by(Specimen_ID, Feather_type)) %>% 
  mutate(Month = str_extract(Collection_date, "(January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|September|October|November|December)")) %>% 
  mutate(location = paste(Specific_collection_location, General_collection_location, sep = ", ")) %>%
  mutate(location = str_replace_all(location, "NA, ", "")) %>% 
  mutate(region = ifelse(region == "Shaft", "Rachis", region))

# Create a data frame 
df <- data.frame(location = dat$location)

# Remove duplicates to avoid redundant API calls
unique_locations <- unique(df$location)

# Get latitude and longitude for unique locations
geocode_data <- geocode(unique_locations)

# Combine the geocode data with the unique locations
location_data <- data.frame(location = unique_locations, geocode_data)

dat <- left_join(dat, location_data, by = "location")

CNOH_dat_meta_full <- readRDS(file = "data/CNOH_museum_samples_201125.rds")
CNOH_dat_meta_full %>% 
  group_by(Specimen_ID, isotope, Feather_type, value) %>% 
  summarise(mean_value = mean(value)) %>% 
  group_by(isotope) %>% 
  summarise(mean_iso_value = mean(mean_value),
            sd_iso_value = sd(mean_value))

barb_shaft_ids <- 
  CNOH_dat_meta_full %>% 
  filter(region != "homogenate") %>% 
  pull(Specimen_ID) %>% 
  unique()

barb_shaft_dat <-
  CNOH_dat_meta_full %>% 
  filter(Specimen_ID %in% barb_shaft_ids) %>% 
  mutate(value_scaled = ifelse(isotope == "normalised_d13c", (value - -18.8) / 3.67,
                        ifelse(isotope == "normalised_d15n", (value - 10.9) / 2.63,
                        ifelse(isotope == "normalised_d18o", (value - 14.1) / 1.39,
                        ifelse(isotope == "normalised_d2h", (value - -21.6) / 22.0, NA))))) %>% 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = isotope,
    values_from = value_scaled
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::select(Specimen_ID, Feather_type, region, normalised_d13c, normalised_d15n) %>% 
    filter(region != "homogenate") %>%
  group_by(Specimen_ID, Feather_type, region) %>%
  summarise(
    normalised_d13c = coalesce(normalised_d13c[!is.na(normalised_d13c)], NA_real_),
    normalised_d15n = coalesce(normalised_d15n[!is.na(normalised_d15n)], NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot() + 
  geom_line(data = barb_shaft_dat,
             aes(x = as.numeric(normalised_d13c), y = as.numeric(normalised_d15n), 
                 color = Feather_type, group = Specimen_ID), 
             size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) + 
  geom_point(data = barb_shaft_dat,
             aes(x = as.numeric(normalised_d13c), y = as.numeric(normalised_d15n), 
                 color = Feather_type, shape = region), 
             size = 3) + 
    # geom_point(data = dat_C_N %>% select(Specimen_ID, avg_d13C_12C, avg_d15N_14N) %>% distinct(),
    #          aes(x = avg_d13C_12C, y = avg_d15N_14N, 
    #              color = Specimen_ID), shape = 3, 
    #          size = 6) + 
  labs(x = expression(delta^{13}*C[scaled]), y = expression(delta^{15}*N[scaled]), shape = "Region",
  color = "Feather") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 15)) +
  luke_theme
```

## Batch 3 and 4 - C, N, & O

### import and wrangle C, N, O, and H data

```{r}
CN_dat <-
  read_excel(here("data/Master_EA_C_N_SI_Batch 3 vane_barb and Batch 4 Breast Feathers_LEH_Sept25_v1_SB.xls"), 
                     sheet = "Sample results_C&N", 
                     col_types = "text", skip = 9, col_names = TRUE) %>%
    rename_with(~ .x %>%
                tolower() %>%
                str_replace_all(" ", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("__", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("___", "_") %>%
                str_replace_all("%", "_perc_") %>%
                str_replace_all(":", "") %>%
                str_replace_all("#", "")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(`identifier_1`, `amount_(mg)`, 
         `ampl_flash_tcd_(mv)...10`, 
         all_correct_amt_perc__n_flash_tcd, 
         `ampl_28_(mv)`, 
         normalised_d15n, 
         `ampl_flash_tcd_(mv)...15`, 
         all_correct_amt_perc__c_flash_tcd, 
         `ampl_44_(mv)`, 
         normalised_d13c, 
         cn_mass_ratio) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(normalised_d15n)) %>%
  rename(amount = `amount_(mg)`) %>% 
  mutate(
    Feather_type = str_extract(identifier_1, regex("Primary|Breast", ignore_case = TRUE)),
```
