```{r}
    d13C = mean(d13C, na.rm = TRUE),
    d15N_scaled = mean(d15N_scaled, na.rm = TRUE),
    d13C_scaled = mean(d13C_scaled, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = feather_type,
              values_from = c(d15N, d13C, d15N_scaled, d13C_scaled),
              names_sep = "_") %>%
  # Derive new useful cols: deltas (breast - primary), and absolute delta
  mutate(
    delta_d15N = d15N_breast - d15N_primary,
    delta_d13C = d13C_breast - d13C_primary,
    abs_delta_d13C = abs(delta_d13C),
    abs_delta_d15N = abs(delta_d15N),
    # keep factor status with resident as reference
    status = factor(status, levels = c("resident", "SI migrant", "NI migrant", "AU migrant"))
  )
# Check number of complete pairs
n_pairs <- df_wide %>% filter(!is.na(d15N_primary) & !is.na(d15N_breast)) %>% nrow()
message("Paired birds: ", n_pairs)

# ---------------------------
# 2. Exploratory plots
# ---------------------------
# Scatter primary vs breast for each isotope; and delta histograms
p1 <- ggplot(df_wide, aes(x = d13C_primary, y = d13C_breast, colour = status)) +
  geom_abline(lty=2) + 
  geom_point(size=3, alpha=0.8) + 
  labs(x="d13C primary (breeding)", y="d13C breast (winter)")

p2 <- ggplot(df_wide, aes(x = d15N_primary, y = d15N_breast, colour = status)) +
  geom_abline(lty=2) + 
  geom_point(size=3, alpha=0.8) + 
  labs(x="d15N primary", y="d15N breast")

p3 <- ggplot(df_wide, aes(x = delta_d13C, fill = status)) + 
  geom_density(alpha=0.4) + 
  labs(x="delta d13C (breast - primary)")

p4 <- ggplot(df_wide, aes(x = delta_d15N, fill = status)) + 
  geom_density(alpha=0.4) + 
  labs(x="delta d15N (breast - primary)")

(p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4)

### box plots of status and feather type ----
# p5 <- 
  ggplot(df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2) %>% filter(status %in% c("AU migrant", "resident")), 
         aes(x = status, y = d15N, fill = feather_type)) + 
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4) +
  theme(#legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# p6 <- 
  ggplot(df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2) %>% filter(status %in% c("AU migrant", "resident")), 
         aes(x = status, y = d13C, fill = feather_type)) + 
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p7 <-
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2), 
            aes(x = x_line, y = d13C, group = ring, color = site_name)) +
  geom_jitter(data = df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2),
              size = 4, aes(x = x_point, y = d13C, fill = site_name),
              shape = 21, alpha = 1, width = 0.075) +
  geom_half_boxplot(data = df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2) %>% filter(feather_type == "primary"), 
                    aes(x = x_box, y = d13C), side = "l") +
  geom_half_boxplot(data = df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2) %>% filter(feather_type == "breast"), 
                    aes(x = x_box, y = d13C), side = "r") +
  facet_grid(~ status, labeller = as_labeller(status_facets)) +
  theme_light() +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 11)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.3, 2.7), breaks = c(1, 2), labels = c("Primary", "Breast")) +
  xlab("Feather tissue") +
  labs(color = NULL, fill = NULL, size = NULL)

p8 <-
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2), 
            aes(x = x_line, y = d15N, group = ring, color = site_name)) +
  geom_jitter(data = df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2),
              size = 4, aes(x = x_point, y = d15N, fill = site_name),
              shape = 21, alpha = 1, width = 0.075) +
  geom_half_boxplot(data = df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2) %>% filter(feather_type == "primary"), 
                    aes(x = x_box, y = d15N), side = "l") +
  geom_half_boxplot(data = df %>% filter(n_tissues == 2) %>% filter(feather_type == "breast"), 
                    aes(x = x_box, y = d15N), side = "r") +
  facet_grid(~ status, labeller = as_labeller(status_facets)) +
  theme_light() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        strip.text = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.3, 2.7), breaks = c(1, 2), labels = c("Primary", "Breast")) +
  xlab("Feather tissue")

(p7 / p8)
```

```{r}
library(MASS)
# Remove rows with any missing isotopes
iso_lda <- df %>%
  drop_na(d15N_scaled, d13C_scaled) %>% 
  mutate(geo_region_bin = ifelse(status == "AU migrant", 1, 0))

# Make geo_region_bin a factor (required by lda)
iso_lda <- iso_lda %>%
  mutate(region = factor(geo_region_bin, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("NZ", "AU")))

# Run LDA
lda_model <- lda(region ~ d13C_scaled + d15N_scaled, data = iso_lda)

# View results
lda_model

# Get predictions
pred <- predict(lda_model)

# Add to dataframe
iso_lda <- iso_lda %>%
  mutate(LD1 = pred$x[, 1],
         predicted = pred$class)

# Confusion matrix
table(Observed = iso_lda$region, Predicted = iso_lda$predicted)

# Classification accuracy
mean(iso_lda$region == iso_lda$predicted)

ggplot(iso_lda, aes(x = LD1, fill = region)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "LDA separation between AU and NZ individuals",
       x = "Linear Discriminant 1", fill = "Region")

ggplot(iso_lda, aes(x = LD1, y = 0, color = region)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0, height = 0.05, size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "LD1", y = NULL, title = "LDA projection")

lda_cv <- lda(region ~ d13C_scaled + d15N_scaled, data = iso_lda, CV = TRUE)

# Accuracy
mean(lda_cv$class == iso_lda$region)

# Confusion matrix
table(Observed = iso_lda$region, Predicted = lda_cv$class)

ggplot(iso_lda, aes(x = region, y = LD1, color = region)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4, shape = 23, fill = "white") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("AU" = "#E69F00", "NZ" = "#56B4E9")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "LDA Separation by Region",
       y = "LD1 score",
       x = "Region")

# Assuming lda_model is already fitted
lda_coeff <- lda_model$scaling[, 1]  # LD1 coefficients
lda_coeff_df <- data.frame(
  isotope = rownames(lda_model$scaling),
  coefficient = lda_coeff
) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(coefficient)))  # order by absolute contribution

ggplot(lda_coeff_df, aes(x = reorder(isotope, coefficient), y = coefficient, fill = coefficient > 0)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#E69F00", "FALSE" = "#56B4E9"), guide = "none") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Contribution of isotopes to LD1",
    x = "Isotope",
    y = "LD1 coefficient"
  )

# --- LDA model (already fitted) ---
lda_model <- lda(region ~ d13C + d15N + d2H + d18O, data = iso_lda)

# --- LD1 scores ---
lda_pred <- predict(lda_model)
lda_df <- data.frame(CNOH_wide, LD1 = lda_pred$x[, 1])

# --- LD1 density plot ---
p1 <- ggplot(iso_lda, aes(x = LD1, fill = region)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("AU" = "#E69F00", "NZ" = "#56B4E9")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "LDA separation by region",
    x = "LD1",
    y = "Density",
    fill = "Region"
  )

# --- Extract LD1 coefficients (loadings) ---
lda_coeff_df <- data.frame(
  isotope = rownames(lda_model$scaling),
  coefficient = lda_model$scaling[, 1]
) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(coefficient)))

# --- Isotope contribution bar plot ---
p2 <- ggplot(lda_coeff_df, aes(x = reorder(isotope, coefficient), y = coefficient, fill = coefficient > 0)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#E69F00", "FALSE" = "#56B4E9"), guide = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Contribution of isotopes to LD1",
    x = "Isotope",
    y = "LD1 coefficient"
  )

# --- Combine plots with patchwork ---
combined_plot <- p1 / p2 + plot_layout(heights = c(2, 1))
combined_plot
```


```{r}
# Also pairwise PCA (optional)
df_pca <- df_wide %>%
  select(d13C_primary, d13C_breast, d15N_primary, d15N_breast) %>%
  na.omit()
pca_res <- prcomp(df_pca %>% scale(), center = TRUE)
autoplot_pca <- function() {
  p <- tibble(pca1 = pca_res$x[,1], pca2 = pca_res$x[,2], status = df_wide$status[!is.na(df_wide$d13C_primary & df_wide$d13C_breast)]) %>%
    ggplot(aes(pca1,pca2,colour=status)) + geom_point(size=3)
  p
}
print(autoplot_pca())

# ---------------------------
# 3. Feature engineering choices
# ---------------------------
# Candidate predictors (try several sets in models):
#  A) primary feather isotopes (d13C_primary, d15N_primary)
#  B) breast feather isotopes (d13C_breast, d15N_breast)
#  C) deltas (delta_d13C, delta_d15N)
#  D) combinations (primary + delta)
# Also consider: sex, breeding longitude/latitude, altitude or site (if you have a site id),
# and interactions (e.g. sex * delta_d13C)
#
# Standardize predictors for models that need it:
num_vars <- c("d13C_primary","d15N_primary","d13C_breast","d15N_breast","delta_d13C","delta_d15N","abs_delta_d13C","abs_delta_d15N")
df_wide <- df_wide %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(num_vars), ~ scale(.)[,1], .names = "z_{col}"))

# ---------------------------
# 4. Cross-validation strategy
# ---------------------------
# --- diagnostics: check site counts first ---
library(dplyr)
n_sites <- df_wide %>% filter(!is.na(delta_d13C)) %>% pull(site) %>% unique() %>% length()
site_counts <- df_wide %>% filter(!is.na(delta_d13C)) %>% count(site, sort = TRUE)

message("Unique sites with paired data: ", n_sites)
print(head(site_counts, 20))

# If you have fewer than 2 sites, grouped CV doesn't make sense:
if(n_sites < 2) stop("Need at least 2 unique sites for grouped CV. Found: ", n_sites)

# choose v sensibly: at most number of sites, default 5 but reduce if needed
v_desired <- 5L
v <- min(v_desired, n_sites)
if(v < v_desired) message("Reduced v to number of sites: v = ", v)

# remove rows missing the predictors you need (e.g. delta_d13C) before cv
df_for_cv <- df_wide %>% filter(!is.na(delta_d13C) & !is.na(d13C_primary) & !is.na(d13C_breast))

# --- Correct usage: pass the column name (unquoted) or as string ---
# Option A: unquoted column name (tidy-eval)
set.seed(123)
group_folds <- rsample::group_vfold_cv(df_for_cv, group = site, v = v)

# Option B: pass column name as a string
# group_folds <- rsample::group_vfold_cv(df_for_cv, group = "site", v = v)

# --- Quick check of folds produced ---
# each element of group_folds is a vfold object; inspect the first split
group_folds$splits[[1]] %>% print()
# get the grouping sites left-out in the first split
first_assessment <- assessment(group_folds$splits[[1]])
leftout_sites <- setdiff(df_for_cv$site, first_assessment$site) # not quite right; better to compute directly:
# better: we can get the analysis set and assessment set and see which sites are in the assessment:
analysis_sites <- unique(analysis(group_folds$splits[[1]])$site)
assessment_sites <- unique(assessment(group_folds$splits[[1]])$site)
message("First split: analysis sites = ", length(analysis_sites), "; assessment sites = ", length(assessment_sites))
message("Example assessment sites (first split): ", paste(head(assessment_sites, 10), collapse = ", "))

# Optional: if some sites have only 1 bird and you want to avoid leaving them alone in assessment,
# consider grouping by larger unit or grouping rare singletons together.

# Print a summary of number of rows in analysis & assessment for each split
fold_summary <- map_dfr(group_folds$splits, ~ tibble(
  id = attr(.x, "id"),
  n_analysis = nrow(analysis(.x)),
  n_assessment = nrow(assessment(.x)),
  n_sites_analysis = n_distinct(analysis(.x)$site),
  n_sites_assessment = n_distinct(assessment(.x)$site)
))
print(fold_summary)

# Because birds may cluster by site, use grouped CV where each fold leaves out entire sites.
# If you have a column for site or geo_region, use that. I'll use longitude_breed rounded as a proxy.
df_wide <- df_wide %>% mutate(site = as.character(site))

set.seed(123)
# grouped 5-fold CV; replace site_group with 'site' if you have it
group_folds <- group_vfold_cv(df_wide %>% filter(!is.na(delta_d13C)), group = df_wide$site, v = 5)

# ---------------------------
# 5. Modeling with tidymodels (logistic, LDA, random forest)
# ---------------------------
# We'll build a function to evaluate models across folds and compute metrics.

# Helper: formula for model (example: using deltas & primary)
model_formula <- status ~ d13C_primary + d15N_primary + delta_d13C + delta_d15N


df_wide$status_bin <- ifelse(df_wide$status == "resident", "resident", "migrant")
df_wide$status_bin <- factor(df_wide$status_bin)

# Prepare recipe
rec <- recipe(status_bin ~ d13C_primary + d15N_primary + delta_d13C + delta_d15N + ring, 
              data = df_wide) %>%
  update_role(ring, new_role = "ID") %>%
  # step_string2factor(sex) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

# Logistic Regression (multinomial if >2 classes)
log_spec <- multinom_reg(mode = "classification") %>%
  set_engine("nnet")   # robust multinomial implementation

# Linear Discriminant Analysis
lda_spec <- discrim_linear() %>%
  set_engine("MASS")

# Random Forest
rf_spec <- rand_forest(
  trees = 1000,
  mtry = 3,
  min_n = 5
) %>% 
  set_mode("classification") %>%
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "impurity")

# logistic workflow
wf_log <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(rec) %>%
  add_model(log_spec)
library(discrim)
# install.packages("discrim")
# LDA workflow
wf_lda <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(rec) %>%
  add_model(lda_spec)

# random forest workflow
wf_rf <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(rec) %>%
  add_model(rf_spec)

# Fit with resampling
set.seed(123)
folds <- vfold_cv(df_wide, v = 10, strata = status_bin)

# install.packages("finetune")
library(finetune)

# Function to evaluate workflows
eval_model <- function(wflow) {
  fit_res <- fit_resamples(
    wflow,
    resamples = folds,
    metrics = metric_set(accuracy, roc_auc),
    control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE)
  )
  return(fit_res)
}
# install.packages("ranger")
library(ranger)
res_log <- eval_model(wf_log)
res_lda <- eval_model(wf_lda)
res_rf  <- eval_model(wf_rf)


log_res <- fit_resamples(wf_log, resamples = group_folds, metrics = metric_set(roc_auc, accuracy, sens, spec), control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE))
lda_res <- fit_resamples(wf_lda, resamples = group_folds, metrics = metric_set(roc_auc, accuracy, sens, spec), control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE))
rf_res  <- fit_resamples(wf_rf,  resamples = group_folds, metrics = metric_set(roc_auc, accuracy, sens, spec), control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE))

collect_metrics(log_res)
collect_metrics(lda_res)
collect_metrics(rf_res)

# Inspect pooled ROC AUC using saved predictions (example for logistic):
log_preds <- collect_predictions(log_res)
roc_auc(log_preds, truth = status, .pred_migrant) # .pred_migrant will exist if glm returns prob for class 'migrant'

# ---------------------------
# 6. Evaluate best model & calibration
# ---------------------------
# Pick winner (e.g. rf_res). Refit on full dataset (or fit a brms GLMM if you want random effects)
best_wf <- wf_rf
final_fit <- last_fit(best_wf %>% update_model(rand_forest(trees = 1000) %>% set_engine("randomForest")), split = initial_split(df_wide %>% filter(!is.na(delta_d13C)), strata = status))
# or simply fit to whole dataset
final_model <- fit(best_wf, data = df_wide)

# Variable importance (for RF)
rf_fit_obj <- extract_fit_parsnip(final_model)$fit
varImp <- importance(rf_fit_obj) %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("variable") %>% arrange(desc(MeanDecreaseGini))
print(varImp)

# Calibration plot for predicted probabilities
preds_all <- predict(final_model, df_wide, type = "prob") %>% bind_cols(df_wide %>% select(status))
preds_all <- preds_all %>% mutate(prob = .pred_migrant)
calib_df <- preds_all %>% mutate(bin = ntile(prob, 10)) %>% group_by(bin) %>% summarise(mean_pred = mean(prob), obs = mean(status == "migrant"), n = n())
ggplot(calib_df, aes(x = mean_pred, y = obs)) + geom_point() + geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, lty=2) + labs(x="Mean predicted prob (migrant)", y="Observed frequency")

# ---------------------------
# 7. Grouped (site-aware) performance summary
# ---------------------------
# It's often useful to show sensitivity/specificity by site (or region)
preds_by_fold <- collect_predictions(rf_res)
# compute confusion matrices per resample
conf_by_split <- preds_by_fold %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(
    cm = list(conf_mat(data = ., truth = status, estimate = .pred_class))
  )
conf_by_split

# ---------------------------
# 8. Bayesian GLMM (optional, for uncertainty & site random effects)
# ---------------------------
# If you want to include random intercept for site and report posterior probability:
# Requires brms installed and a working backend; this can be slow but gives full posterior probs.

# Create site factor (replace with real site ID if available)
df_wide <- df_wide %>% mutate(site = site_group)
brms_formula <- bf(as.integer(status=="migrant") ~ d13C_primary + d15N_primary + delta_d13C + delta_d15N + sex + (1|site))
# Fit (short chains for example)
# fit_brms <- brm(brms_formula, data = df_wide, family = bernoulli(link="logit"), chains=4, cores=4, iter=2000)
# After fit, get posterior predicted probability for new observations with posterior_predict or posterior_linpred (type="response")

# ---------------------------
# 9. Predict function for a new bird
# ---------------------------
# Assume you will supply d13C_primary, d15N_primary, d13C_breast, d15N_breast (or deltas). This returns predicted probability of migrant.

predict_migration <- function(model_fit, newdata_df){
  # newdata_df should have columns: d13C_primary, d15N_primary, d13C_breast, d15N_breast, sex (if used)
  newdata_df <- newdata_df %>%
    mutate(delta_d13C = d13C_breast - d13C_primary,
           delta_d15N = d15N_breast - d15N_primary)
  probs <- predict(model_fit, newdata_df, type = "prob")
  # return dataframe with prob of migrant
  bind_cols(newdata_df, probs)
}

# Example usage:
# newbird <- tibble(d13C_primary = -21.0, d15N_primary = 12.3, d13C_breast = -17.0, d15N_breast = 14.1, sex = "F")
# predict_migration(final_model, newbird)

# ---------------------------
# 10. Thresholding & reporting
# ---------------------------
# Choose a classification threshold depending on your goal:
# - If missing a migrant is costly, choose threshold to maximize sensitivity.
# - If false positives are costly, maximize specificity.
# Use ROC curve to pick threshold:
rf_preds <- collect_predictions(rf_res)
roc_obj <- roc(response = rf_preds$truth, predictor = rf_preds$.pred_migrant)
coords(roc_obj, x="best", best.method = "youden") # returns threshold maximizing sensitivity+specificity

# ---------------------------
# 11. Quick realism assessment
# ---------------------------
# After running the models, assess:
# - AUC > 0.80 => good discrimination (screening feasible),
# - 0.7 < AUC < 0.8 => moderate (useful as a triage tool),
# - AUC < 0.7 => poor for single-bird classification.
# Also inspect confusion matrix, and site-wise performance. If performance differs strongly among sites, you may need site-specific models or include spatial covariates.

# ---------------------------
# 12. Sensitivity checks & suggestions
# ---------------------------
# - Try simpler rule-based classifier: e.g., if delta_d13C > X then migrant. Use ROC to pick X.
# - Test models restricted to alpine or coastal birds to see if patterns differ.
# - If you have sample-age or feather molt timing metadata, include it.
# - External validation: ideally test on an independent holdout of birds not used in training.
# - Bayesian GLMM recommended if you want full posterior uncertainty and to borrow strength across sites.
# - Beware possible trophic or dietary differences (d15N) that might confound movement signal; interpret with ecology in mind.

# End of pipeline
message("Pipeline script executed. Fit models and inspect metrics to judge feasibility.")

```
